questions.explanation: The Growth stage is characterized by rapid adoption, scaling channels, and increasing market share after initial validation.
questions.question: In the product life-cycle, which stage typically focuses on scaling distribution and increasing market share after product-market fit is established?

questions.explanation: A product roadmap aligns stakeholders on priorities, sequencing, and rationale for planned work.
questions.question: Which artifact most directly communicates what will be built, why, and in what order over time?

questions.explanation: An event taxonomy standardizes how user actions are tracked (names, properties, conventions) to ensure reliable analysis.

options.A: Defining consistent event names, properties, and rules for analytics instrumentation
options.C: Choosing the database engine for the data warehouse
options.D: Designing UI color palettes and typography

questions.question: In event tracking, what is an 'event taxonomy' primarily used for?

questions.explanation: POST is commonly used to create a new resource on the server.
questions.question: Which HTTP method is most appropriate for creating a new resource in a REST API?

questions.explanation: A good KPI is specific, behavior-based, and time-bounded; completing onboarding plus a key action is a clear activation signal.

options.A: Activation = users who 'seem engaged' in the first week
options.B: Activation = users who open the app at least once
options.C: Activation = users who complete onboarding and perform the key action within 24 hours (e.g., create first project)

questions.question: You want to measure activation for a new onboarding flow. Which KPI definition is most actionable and least ambiguous?

questions.explanation: A proof-of-concept prototype tests whether the core technology can meet requirements, reducing feasibility risk early.

options.B: Building a proof-of-concept prototype and validating performance against target specs

questions.question: In industrial R&D for a physical product, which activity best reduces technical feasibility risk before committing to full-scale development?

questions.explanation: WAU requires grouping by week and counting distinct users, with a time filter that captures the last 4 full weeks.

options.A: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE event_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days';
options.B: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', event_time) AS wk, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS wau FROM events WHERE event_time >= DATE_TRUNC('week', NOW()) - INTERVAL '4 weeks' AND event_time < DATE_TRUNC('week', NOW()) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;
options.C: SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) FROM events GROUP BY user_id;
options.D: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', event_time) AS day, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM events GROUP BY 1;

questions.question: You have a table events(user_id, event_name, event_time). Which SQL query best calculates weekly active users (WAU) for the last 4 full weeks?

questions.explanation: When instrumentation changes, measurement may be biased; validating against backend records and auditing event definitions reduces false conclusions.

options.A: Announce the win and roll out globally immediately
options.B: Compare conversion using an independent source of truth (e.g., backend user_created records) and audit event definitions before interpreting the lift
options.C: Ignore the instrumentation change because the trend is positive
options.D: Switch analytics tools (e.g., from Amplitude to GA) to confirm

questions.question: After launching a new signup flow, conversion appears to increase by 5%. However, you also changed the event instrumentation at launch. What is the best next step to validate the result?

questions.explanation: When metrics move in opposite directions, a synthesized business metric like revenue per visitor plus uncertainty and guardrails provides a sound decision basis.

options.A: Choose the variant with the higher p-value because it is safer
options.B: Decide based only on conversion rate because it is the primary metric
options.C: Compute expected revenue per visitor (conversion rate × average order value) and evaluate statistical uncertainty and guardrails (e.g., refunds, latency)
options.D: Stop the test early as soon as one metric looks better

questions.question: You run an A/B test on a checkout change. Variant B increases average order value but decreases conversion rate. Which approach best supports a product decision?

questions.explanation: Usability testing on prototypes validates workflows and comprehension early, catching issues before engineering investment and reducing rework.

options.A: Skip testing until after launch to avoid slowing down delivery
options.B: Run moderated usability tests on an interactive prototype with representative users and clear task success criteria
options.C: Ask internal stakeholders to vote on the best design in a meeting
options.D: Only test the final build with automated unit tests

questions.question: A team is developing a new product design and wants to reduce late-stage rework. Which testing strategy is most effective before full implementation?
